issues: small sprite footprint reduces sub-0.5s spottability
positioned at boundary between dark red-tile zone and mid-tone open area — moderate contrast only
white boxy silhouette could be confused with player character at a glance
reasoning: The echo_box_idle_v07 sprite (white pixel-art cube character at ~(635,340) in composite) is absent in the original and clearly composited in. Pros: white/light coloring provides reasonable contrast against the dark tile floor; pixel-art style is consistent with other game entities and reads as native, not pasted; silhouette is boxy and distinct from organic bg tile shapes. Cons: the sprite is small (~30px), making instant 0.5s locatability borderline; it sits at a lighting boundary (dark red area edge to open mid-tone area) so contrast is inconsistent depending on exact tile underneath; its white boxy form is close in read to the player character sprite also present in the scene, creating mild entity confusion risk. No outline disappearance or tonal clash with the Echoes palette, but overall integration is adequate rather than strong — qualifies as marginal.
